disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Sudden onset of severe lower back pain with a felt 'pop' during a manual lift/transfer
trigger_question_examples: What were you doing when the pain started?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I was lifting and transferring the person I care for and I felt a pop in my lower back, then it seized up and I went down.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Complete inability to stand or weight-bear because of pain and spasm
trigger_question_examples: Can you stand or walk at all?
How is it affecting your movement?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No — I can't get up off the floor. Every time I try to move my back locks up completely.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No saddle numbness, no loss of bladder or bowel control, and can still feel and move both feet and toes (cauda equina red-flag screen — negative on patient-reportable features)
trigger_question_examples: Any numbness around your buttocks, groin or inner thighs?
Have you lost any control of your bladder or bowels?
Can you still feel and move your feet and toes normally?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No numbness down there, no accidents with my bladder or bowels, and I can still wiggle and feel my toes fine.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Pain is mechanical (worse on any movement, eased by lying still), not colicky or radiating to the groin, and no fever reported
trigger_question_examples: Does anything make it better or worse?
Any fever, or pain spreading into your groin?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's worst when I try to move and eases if I stay dead still. It's not a wave-like pain and it's not going into my groin. No fever.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Months of ignored back-strain warning signs and years of round-the-clock unpaid caregiving with severe exhaustion, sleep deprivation and no support
trigger_question_examples: Has your back been bothering you before today?
Can you tell me about what's been going on for you day to day?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My back's been warning me for months but I couldn't stop — I care for my family member alone, all day and night, barely sleeping, with no help at all.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: A highly dependent family member relies on the patient for all care and is now without support because the patient has collapsed
trigger_question_examples: Is anyone else there with you?
Is anyone depending on you right now?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: There's no one else — the person I care for depends on me for everything and now I can't even get up to help them.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Overwhelming guilt, resentment and exhaustion; denies any thoughts of self-harm or of harming the care receiver when sensitively asked
trigger_question_examples: This sounds exhausting — how are you coping emotionally?
Have you had any thoughts of harming yourself or that you can't go on?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I feel so guilty and worn down and angry that no one helps — but no, I haven't had thoughts of hurting myself or anyone else.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I'm just tired, that's all — can we focus on my back?
